note: This is a single-box environment (not a separate 'remote' host reachable over SSH from the assistant) -- the assistant's Bash tool runs directly on this machine, which happens to have a GPU. Treated as 'the remote GPU server' for this benchmark per task framing.

gpu.vram_used_note: baseline usage from Xorg + gnome-remote-desktop processes already resident; not IRIS. Effective free VRAM for IRIS workloads is lower than the nominal 24564 MiB by that amount.

disk.flag: Only 92G free. Full NExT-GQA video corpus (990 test + 567 val videos, raw NExT-QA video source) is commonly 100GB+. This is a hard constraint on Part 2/4 scope -- must be checked before bulk download, may require downloading a working subset or streaming/deleting after per-video processing rather than caching everything at once.

key_packages.full_pip_freeze_note: python -m pip freeze captured to /tmp/pip_freeze.txt this session (315 packages); NOTE: the plain `pip` on PATH resolves to /home/ccbd/.local/bin/pip, a DIFFERENT environment (reported torch==2.8.0) than the miniconda python actually used to run IRIS (torch==2.11.0). Always invoke `python -m pip` on this box, never bare `pip`, or package versions will be misreported.

ffmpeg.h264_mv_export_support: not independently verified in this pass; prior repo docs (FINAL_PRE_BENCHMARK_VERIFICATION) note PyAV/ffmpeg with H.264 MV export is required for real-video ingest and the one real-video codec test skips without a fixture -- re-verify with an actual video before trusting motion-vector-derived geometry features on this box

clip_embedding_backend.configured_model: ViT-B/32 (config.clip_model default None -> falls back to clip_revision)
clip_embedding_backend.loadable: not independently smoke-tested in this pass yet

captioner.resolution_mechanism: MiniCPMCaptioner (iris/aria.py) auto-detects model name from the OpenAI-compatible /v1/models list at answerer_endpoint; prefers 'minicpm-v4.6:latest'/'minicpm-v4.6', else falls back to 'minicpm-v:latest'/'minicpm-v'
captioner.actually_available_on_this_box: minicpm-v:latest (NOT minicpm-v4.6) -- confirmed via GET http://127.0.0.1:8091/v1/models
captioner.deviation: DOCUMENTED DEVIATION: prior repo decisions (DECISIONS.md) seat 'minicpm-v4.6-inventory' as the production captioner. This box only has the plain 'minicpm-v:latest' tag loaded on the llama_server instance, not minicpm-v4.6. MiniCPMCaptioner's own fallback logic will silently use minicpm-v instead of minicpm-v4.6 -- captions in this run come from minicpm-v, not minicpm-v4.6. This is a real model substitution, not a hypothetical -- record it against every output file produced by this run.

answerer.reachability: REACHABLE. curl http://127.0.0.1:8091/v1/models returned 200 with models: minicpm-v:latest, granite4:micro, llama3.2:latest.
answerer.conclusion: The configured llama_server default IS live and usable on this box as-is. No Ollama fallback substitution is needed here (unlike the CPU smoke runs referenced in EXPERIMENT_LOG.md, which used Ollama on a VRAM-constrained box). Server appears to be either llama.cpp's llama-server or an Ollama instance exposing an OpenAI-compatible /v1 route on port 8091 (not independently distinguished in this pass -- the /v1/models payload's 'owned_by':'library' field is consistent with either); noted as an open item, does not change functional reachability.

nli_verifier.status_as_first_observed: BROKEN via default online path: huggingface_hub.errors.RepositoryNotFoundError / '401 Client Error... OAuth token signature verification failed' when the loader calls the HF Hub API. Reproduced identically on HEAD before any Part-1 edit (git stash verified). Root cause isolated: ~/.cache/huggingface/token holds a stale/invalid cached OAuth token (whoami() -> 'Invalid user token'), so every HF Hub API call 401s, EVEN THOUGH the model weights are already present in the local cache (~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--cross-encoder--nli-deberta-v3-base).
nli_verifier.fix_applied: Documented environment-level workaround (no code change): set HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 in the process environment for benchmark runs. This makes transformers/huggingface_hub skip the Hub API entirely and load straight from local cache, which succeeded (AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained loaded cleanly). All Part 1+ runs on this box that touch cerberus_mode='v2' verification are executed with HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 set. cerberus_mode='none' runs never touch this model at all, so they are unaffected either way.
nli_verifier.recommendation: The stale token at ~/.cache/huggingface/token should eventually be refreshed or removed by whoever owns this box; HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 is a workaround for this benchmark session, not a permanent fix.

hf_hub_download_for_dataset_fetch.issue: The same stale ~/.cache/huggingface/token also breaks anonymous huggingface_hub.hf_hub_download() calls against public dataset repos (e.g. VLM2Vec/nextqa-rawvideo) -- HEAD requests 401 with 'OAuth token signature verification failed' when the stale token is attached.
hf_hub_download_for_dataset_fetch.fix_applied: Pass token=False explicitly to every hf_hub_download()/snapshot_download() call in this benchmark (verified working: downloaded 6936757706.mp4, 3079724515.mp4, 8900428927.mp4 from VLM2Vec/nextqa-rawvideo this way). HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 does NOT help here since these files were not yet cached. This must be applied consistently for the full Part 2 dataset download (990+567 videos) or downloads will fail the same way.

latency_scope_warning: ALL latency numbers produced by any run on this environment are GPU-box numbers (RTX 4090, 32-thread i9-13900, 125GB RAM, local NVMe). They must never be used, cited, or extrapolated to support any CPU-only or edge-device latency claim. Every output file in this benchmark that reports latency must carry this same warning inline.
